reviews.review_description:
The Deagle has landed. Great app. Excellent app. Award winning profound visceral quality. Top tier. A real gem. I can't believe the this is free. I wish two handed guns was an option. Excellent. Profound. Shimmering. Sterling silver. Platinum grade. Par excellence.
If Deagle Jack was a restaurant item, this would win Michelin stars. Spackling. Smokin'!!!!

Please add gun angle (pitch) and gun yaw options so you can use an external gun (k3d or Ace gun) not just the quest controller, this will make the game experience better! These games already support it: Speed ​​shooting, Dead second, Phistol whip, AIM assault, Augmented defender, Hyper bullet

developer_response.body:
A 1-star review + a wishlist is a bold combo, not gonna lie.

Adding gun pitch/yaw support for external gunstocks is definitely a cool idea, and I get why you'd want it. But usually, giving the game the same score as a toaster on fire isn’t the fastest way to inspire a developer to jump joyfully into new feature implementation.

That said, I did read your feedback, and I’ll keep it in consideration for future updates.

For now, Deagle Jack was designed around the Quest controllers — but who knows, maybe one day it’ll become best friends with your K3D too.

reviews.review_description: This game creaps up on you, i played it once and thought it was pretty fun and went on checking out games... But keep coming back to this game. Because it is just what it is and it does that well. Sure they could put pay to win powerups in or just monetize it in a bunch of in game ways. Its so much more because they didnt, like they made something they wanted to play. I have not clocked it yet and wont give spoilers. And for all i know they turn up later in the game but wow have i wished i had an area denial consumable a few times after not managing the hoard properly. Not fire for obvious reasons but magic dust or iron powder or something. Maybe as one of the cards? or maybe they tried it and it just ruined the experience somehow. Ok its late im rambling. Try this game

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for taking the time to write this — it really means a lot to me.

I’m glad the game “crept up on you” in that way. What you described is exactly the experience I wanted to create: a game I personally wanted to play, without pay-to-win mechanics or forced monetization. Hearing that this comes through makes all the effort worth it.

I also wanted to let you know that a new update is now available, with lots of new features and new levels added to the game.

The game will become paid once it leaves Early Access, so if you download it now, you’ll keep it forever for free in your library 🙂

Thanks again for the support, and for sharing your thoughts and ideas — they’re always appreciated.

reviews.review_description: This is major league cool and a freaky experience.  This is hands down the best free game I’ve ever played I would pay for this experience.  I hope the developers bring this to a full game status and give us more.  ADDICTIVE as hell chasing that next best score and honing your skills, hoping to see Casper the friendly white ghost to give me some free upgrades along the way!!  Graphics are cool, enemies are something out of a Tim Burton movie mixed with some stranger things( I swear one of them looks like a demigorgon) and the whole experience is just fun.  Thank you for the gift of sharing your amazing game with me.  Very cool.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! I'm happy you’re enjoying DEAGLE JACK. I’ve actually added some new levels recently, so there’s even more chaos and fun to dive into. Your support and enthusiasm mean a lot!

reviews.review_description: What a fun, nutty blast. So many cool little surprises… quite imaginative. Just played today for the first time and can’t stop thinking about playing it tomorrow. Thanks devs…! Ps. I would have totally paid for this, so thanks for making it free.

Cant wait for more Deagle Jack content!! Other than you’re using a beretta m9 instead of a Deagle! Aside from that non intrusive issue the game runs and looks great, id like to see a pause menu and maybe a skill tree of what you have selected each round ,that would be awesome! This game is fun AF! Good graphics, smooth gameplay, intense wave blasting action!

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot, really appreciate the support!
And yeah — totally caught on the M9 thing 😅 it's on the list!
The skill tree (or at least a clear way to track your power-ups each round) is one of the next priorities — I 100% agree it would add a lot.

reviews.review_description: My jaw dropped when I saw the graphics. Photo realistic on the Quest 3? Great game play, slowly getting more difficult. How did they do this when big developers are still producing ps2 level graphics. Support this developer, they could be the future of Quest 3 graphics we all want.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the awesome feedback!

The realistic graphics in Deagle Jack are powered by Oniri, a new tech developed by Oneiros—a team I’m part of as one of the founding admins. Oniri makes it possible to push visual quality on the Quest 3 way beyond what people expect from standalone VR.

There are lots of cool ideas and future projects lined up to take full advantage of it, but it all depends on how Deagle Jack is received by players like you. Your feedback means a lot and helps shape the future.

Thanks again for the amazing review!

reviews.review_description:
This is easily the single best free game I've ever come across here, or even on Sidequest for that matter.   It's absolutely worth downloading to see for yourself.   The graphics alone make it checking out but it's really all about the expertly administered difficulty curve that will have you feeling very confident initially only to slowly begin to tighten the screws until, before you know it, you've gotten in over your head.   This never leaves you feeling overwhelmed though and always has you feeling confident that the next round will be the one, which is a hallmark of a truly great game.   

As far as arcade shooters go, this really is one of the best experiences available in all of VR and I think this is on account of it embracing a very simplistic and satisfying approach with it's mechanics that function so well that the experience really never becomes stale as a result and will have you returning to beat a previous score or to make it just a little further than you did the last time.  

There really isn't enough good that can be said for the difficulty curve in this game.    It's nearly perfect and makes this a tremendously addictive shooter and an absolute no brainer at it's current price.   

One last note, the developer is very friendly and responsive and this is directly reflected in the quality of the game.  I was truly impressed to see suggestions I'd made not only be received kindly but literally become implemented into the experience literally hours after communicating them.  Just pure class and needed to be pointed out as it's not always the case that you'll encounter this.   

Definitely download this and check it out for yourself.   I'm looking forward to whatever incarnation this takes on when it's expanded and will happily pay for that when it arrives but definitely feel that there's enough here currently to warrant downloading it and would be hard pressed to think of or recommend a better arcade shooter, regardless of price.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your incredible comment — it really means a lot to me.

And wow… I just saw you're now at the top of the global leaderboard! What an amazing achievement. You even beat my record — respect!

As soon as I turn on the air conditioning, I’ll be back in the game, doing everything I can to take that spot back. 😄

Thanks again for your kind words and support — players like you are what keep this project alive and evolving!

reviews.review_title: Clearly WIP-But oh I love it already!
reviews.review_description: I got my Q2 for Beat Saber only really.. But this popped right up at me, and I noticed the Torii gate - so got me interested.. Next thing I know, I'm at the end of horde6 hands trembling. Occasionally would hope for a bit tighter gun control (even though I think I'm accurate, it takes even the slightest shake and misses :D). Haven't seen all the perks so hoping to see DUAL WIELD (or maybe game mode with double the fun? ;) ). Also manual loading / clip drop animation or something in the pipeline? All-in-all 6/5 game already and I'm hoping for a great future. Need to do a Tiktok video of this or something - people need to see this!

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the feedback! I really appreciate your suggestions and will definitely keep them in mind for future updates. I'm currently working on new levels and game modes, and there's a lot more coming soon!

And yes – a TikTok video would be absolutely amazing! Every bit of help in spreading the word really means a lot and is essential for the future development of the game. Thanks again for playing and for the support!

reviews.review_description:
It’s decent. A cool laxed game, but it has some challenge to it. But I have some complaints.

The first thing that bugged me tremendously was the fact that despite what the title and description would have you believe you don’t in fact play with a Desert Eagle, you play with a Beretta M9 with a laser attachment. 

Second was the graphics. I saw Oniri and got excited about it especially seeing it came out just a month ago and was hyped to see it, as the forest demo looked really good on Q3 even though it was Q2 optimized. I know the quest isnt that powerful but a bump in resolution would be nice.

The third and final was the zombies, they’re very basic which isn’t a bad thing, but they are a bit slow. I found myself being able to shoot them before they get close to me and wait for the next horde for a few seconds. Maybe make them a bit faster? 

I’m not too mad about this game, it’s free so I can’t complain too much overall a cool experience for players of the younger variety.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks a lot for your feedback!
About the Desert Eagle — the game is still in early access, so you get the trusty Beretta for now. Honestly, I didn’t think it would bother anyone that much, but don’t worry: it will definitely be replaced in the full version!
Regarding the resolution: I could use higher resolution assets, but that would make the build much heavier to download and install. For now, I think it’s a fair compromise.
And about the zombies — their speed is intentional. Instead of making them faster, I prefer to increase their number and spawn frequency. Trust me, at higher levels it gets pretty intense!
I appreciate you playing and sharing your thoughts — hope you keep having fun!

reviews.review_description: This is crazy awesome! Quest 3's standalone performance, realistic graphics and delicate mapping details show the best possibilities and the future of meta quest games. And it's free!!!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! Really glad you enjoyed Deagle Jack. It’s the flagship title showcasing Oniri technology. If Deagle Jack proves successful, there’s a good chance other games will start adopting it too. Your support truly means a lot!
